content.text:
Looking at the new transcripts, I'm seeing a mix of user reports and empty commits. There's an e2e test error about MFA challenge URLs, a design review issue around button hover color migration, and a couple of `/commit-changes` entries with no actual content. The last entry appears to be cut off mid-thought.

I'm noticing the auth app architecture detail about being agnostic of consumer apps is substantive enough to matter for future changes, but it's only appearing in one transcript so far. The other issues—the linting recursion with turborepo, the build error with the SDK, the design token mapping—these all look like one-off problems or workflow steps that are already covered by existing documentation. I don't see a pattern of recurring corrections that would warrant adding to the workspace facts beyond what's already in AGENTS.md. I'm not finding any high-signal recurring corrections across the transcripts I've reviewed, so there's nothing to add to memory. The auth app's generic design principle is already being captured in the docs and code being added this session. Now I'm verifying that all the indexed transcript files still exist on disk, and they're all checking out. I need to pull the current modification times in milliseconds for those 10 processed files so I can update the index with accurate timestamps.

input.description: Get precise mtimeMs for processed transcripts

content.text:
Based on my review, none of the new/updated transcripts contain high-signal recurring user corrections or new durable workspace facts that aren't already captured. Specifically:

- 5 transcripts are pure workflow invocations (`/commit-changes`, `/review-un-staged`)
- 4 transcripts are one-off debugging/review requests (MFA e2e flake, button hover token regression, turborepo recursion, missing peer dep)
- 1 transcript (`e0c1bd5a`) is a single-instance architectural directive (keep the auth app agnostic across consuming apps) which is being captured directly in this branch's new docs/code (`apps/auth/docs/app-integration.md`, `app-integration.ts`), not a recurring correction

I'll leave `AGENTS.md` unchanged and only refresh the index.

 I'm reviewing the findings from the transcript analysis. There are five items total: one MFA challenge bug that was a one-off issue, two instances where the `/commit-changes` workflow was the only factor, a design token hover color migration regression, and one more item that appears to be cut off.

Looking at the rest of the data, I see there are actually eight items total across the transcripts, with most being isolated issues or workflow-specific. The only significant pattern is an architectural directive about keeping the auth app agnostic to consuming apps, which is being documented directly in the codebase rather than in AGENTS.md. Since none of these corrections recur across multiple transcripts and the architectural guidance is already being captured in the new docs, AGENTS.md doesn't need updates.

For the transcript index, I need to refresh the modification times for the two reprocessed entries and add eight new entries for the newly analyzed transcripts, keeping all existing entries intact.

Writing the updated index file...

No high-signal memory updates.

Index housekeeping performed:
- Refreshed mtimes for the 2 transcripts that had grown since last index (`a8548671*` parent + its `c93f1525` subagent).
- Added 8 new entries: `582192e2`, `bce92bf5`, `7b19be6c`, `b2ba055e`, `569a1e90`, `16092574`, `e0c1bd5a` parent + its `1c10d151` subagent.
- Removed 0 entries (every previously indexed transcript still exists on disk).
- All other 36 indexed entries were preserved with their existing mtimes (file mtimes unchanged, so not reprocessed).

`AGENTS.md` was left unchanged. The new transcripts were either pure workflow invocations (`/commit-changes`, `/review-un-staged`), one-off debugging/review tasks, or a single-instance architectural directive (auth app should stay agnostic to consuming apps) that is being captured directly in this branch's new `apps/auth/docs/app-integration.md` and `apps/auth/src/lib/app-integration.ts` rather than as durable memory.
